payload.text:
You are a senior full-stack engineer + UI/UX designer + SEO specialist.
Build a production-ready website called "ThumbSnap.io" — a YouTube Thumbnail Downloader.

GOAL:
A fast, modern, SEO-optimized tool that lets users paste a YouTube URL and instantly download ALL available thumbnail formats/qualities with one-click buttons. The site must look visually similar to the reference design (same layout vibe: top nav, big hero banner, central input, green download button, simple cards below) but with a more premium, clean, modern finish.

TECH REQUIREMENTS:
- Use Next.js (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS.
- Server-side API route to parse YouTube video ID and fetch thumbnails.
- No external paid APIs. Only use public YouTube thumbnail URL patterns.
- Must be responsive (mobile-first), fast, accessible, and robust.
- Add rate limiting + basic anti-abuse.
- Add loading states, error handling, and clean UX.

CORE FEATURE:
1) INPUT:
- A single input field where user pastes a YouTube link (supports youtu.be, youtube.com/watch?v=, shorts, embed links).
- A large green primary button: "Get Thumbnails" (Arabic: "جلب الصور المصغرة").

2) OUTPUT:
After fetching the video ID, show a results section with:
- Video title (optional if can be fetched without official API; if not, omit title).
- A grid/table of all thumbnail formats/qualities, each row includes:
  - Preview image
  - Label (e.g., "maxresdefault", "sddefault", "hqdefault", "mqdefault", "default", and also i.ytimg.com/vi_webp variants if available)
  - Dimensions (if known; otherwise display "Unknown")
  - File type badge: JPG / WEBP
  - "Download" button for each item (direct download)
  - "Open" button (opens in new tab)
- Include a "Download All" option (zip) ONLY if feasible without heavy server costs; otherwise provide “Open All” and explain "download individually".
- Show message if some qualities are not available for a video (common case): "Some qualities are not available for this video."

THUMBNAIL URLS:
Generate and test these patterns:
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{VIDEO_ID}/maxresdefault.jpg
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{VIDEO_ID}/sddefault.jpg
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{VIDEO_ID}/hqdefault.jpg
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{VIDEO_ID}/mqdefault.jpg
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi/{VIDEO_ID}/default.jpg
Also WEBP variants:
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/{VIDEO_ID}/maxresdefault.webp
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/{VIDEO_ID}/sddefault.webp
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/{VIDEO_ID}/hqdefault.webp
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/{VIDEO_ID}/mqdefault.webp
- https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/{VIDEO_ID}/default.webp
Before showing a thumbnail row, validate existence with a HEAD request (server-side) to avoid broken options and show only what exists.

MULTI-LANGUAGE (IMPORTANT):
- Must support Arabic (RTL) and English (LTR) plus additional languages.
- Detect device/browser language automatically and set default language.
- Allow manual switching from the navbar.
- Implement i18n with JSON dictionaries.
- At minimum: Arabic + English fully translated.
- Arabic UI must be RTL with correct alignment, typography and spacing.

PAGES:
- Home (tool)
- Privacy Policy (required)
- Terms of Service (required)
- Contact page (simple)
- FAQ page
- Blog index (optional for SEO growth; at least 3 starter articles)

SEO REQUIREMENTS:
- Perfect on-page SEO:
  - Title tags, meta descriptions, OpenGraph, Twitter cards
  - Canonical URLs
  - JSON-LD structured data: WebSite + FAQPage + SoftwareApplication
  - Sitemap.xml (dynamic)
  - robots.txt
- SEO-friendly URL structure:
  - /ar and /en routes
- Fast performance, Core Web Vitals optimized.
- Add internal links to FAQ, Privacy, Terms.
- Add schema for “YouTube Thumbnail Downloader”.

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS (match reference vibe):
- Top navbar with links similar to: Home, YouTube Thumbnail, YouTube Tools (dropdown), Blog, FAQ, Privacy.
- Hero section with:
  - Big site name (ThumbSnap.io)
  - Subtitle: “Download YouTube Thumbnails in HD”
  - Input + green button centered
- Background with subtle icons pattern (like media icons) but modern.
- Below hero, show “Related Tools” cards (even if not implemented) for SEO and expansion:
  - YouTube Title Generator
  - YouTube Tags Extractor
  - YouTube Channel Banner Size Guide
  - YouTube Shorts Thumbnail Tips
Each card has icon + title + short description + “Open” button.

ADS MONETIZATION (SAFE):
- Prepare ad placeholders (do not load ads by default in dev):
  - One horizontal banner below hero
  - One sidebar sticky ad on desktop
  - One in-content ad between sections
- Ensure layout doesn’t break without ads.
- Add a consent-friendly cookie banner (simple).
- Avoid intrusive popups.

RECOMMENDED AD NETWORKS (content-safe):
- Google AdSense (primary)
- Ezoic (for later when traffic grows)
- Media.net (alternative)
- PropellerAds / Adsterra only as optional fallback (keep disabled by default)

CONTENT POLICY & LEGAL:
- This site is only for downloading publicly available YouTube thumbnail images.
- Add disclaimer in footer: “ThumbSnap.io is not affiliated with YouTube.”
- Respect copyright: add a short note advising users to only use thumbnails they have rights to.

DELIVERABLES:
- Provide the full project code structure
- Provide all pages content (EN + AR)
- Provide i18n JSON files
- Provide SEO files: sitemap, robots, metadata
- Provide deployment instructions (Vercel preferred)
- Ensure no runtime errors, clean TypeScript, and strong error handling.

FINAL QUALITY BAR:
- Must look professional, fast, and stable.
- No broken links, no broken RTL layout.
- Clean UI like the reference but improved.

**Designing the Thumbnail Downloader**

Okay, I'm starting to visualize "ThumbSnap.io." My initial focus is on the core functionality, the downloader itself. Next.js App Router seems like the right choice. I envision a clean, green, premium-looking UI with Tailwind CSS. I'm also thinking of how to incorporate SEO best practices from the outset to make this thing discoverable. English and Arabic support are also in the planning.


**Analyzing Technical Implementation**

I've clarified the tool's core: paste YouTube URL, get thumbnails in JPG/WEBP, one-click download. Technical aspects are solidifying, leaning towards a React SPA with `HashRouter` to align with constraints, and TypeScript, to ensure type safety. I'm focusing on server-side thumbnail validation via HEAD requests to avoid paid APIs. I'm also ensuring a premium user experience with responsive design, loading states, and RTL support.


**Detailing App Structure**

I'm now diving into the specifics of the app's structure. I'm planning out the file organization, considering components like Navbar, Hero, and Footer. I've sketched the use of fetch with 'HEAD' to validate thumbnails. I'm focusing on the multilingual and RTL implementation. For the multilingual aspect, I'm thinking of a context provider or a simple state management. The RTL is going to use the "dir=rtl" attribute based on the language.


**Expanding Feature Scope**

I'm now adding a bit more detail to the visual design, like a premium shadow for a large, green button. I'm also planning the results grid as cards with preview, details, and download buttons. For SEO, I'm thinking of dynamic meta tags or a dedicated component. Although it's an SPA, I'll provide the content for a sitemap, focusing on internal navigation in the code. I'm also adding a blog with sample articles. I'll inject JSON-LD into the head. I'm pondering how to add the "Gemini-powered Thumbnail Optimizer" tip generator to enhance the product.


**Defining Core Implementation Details**

I'm making progress. I've chosen `dir="rtl"` to ensure RTL support and am setting the correct text alignment. I can now fetch video titles directly from YouTube's oEmbed endpoint without an API key, which is great. I'll make sure to use suitable Arabic fonts, such as Tajawal or Almarai. I'll also pre-generate `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`. I'm starting to build, keeping the file structure in mind: `index.html` loads Tailwind, `constants.ts` handles translations, `youtubeService.ts` gets video data, and `App.tsx` handles routing.

entries.description: Project metadata including name, description, and required permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Google Fonts.
TypeScript definitions for the application.
Application constants and translations.
Utility functions to handle YouTube ID extraction and metadata fetching.
Component to toggle between English and Arabic.
Mockup for advertisement slots.
Main application logic and layout.
Entry point for rendering the React application.
SEO robots configuration.

<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://thumbsnap.io/</loc>
    <lastmod>2024-05-20</lastmod>
    <changefreq>daily</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://thumbsnap.io/#faq</loc>
    <priority>0.8</priority>
  </url>
  <url>
    <loc>https://thumbsnap.io/#privacy</loc>
    <priority>0.5</priority>
  </url>
</urlset>

payload.text: حول هذه الموقع لكود بلوجر كامل بدون زياده او نقصان

**Defining the Conversion Goal**

Okay, so I'm aiming to transform ThumbSnap.io's React-based front-end into a single Blogger template. I need to ensure it's production-ready, lightning-fast, modern, and SEO-optimized, with multilingual support for Arabic and English, including RTL display. This is quite the challenge!


**Mapping the Component Flow**

I'm now focusing on the architectural translation. I have identified the key files: App.tsx, youtubeService.ts, constants.ts, index.html, and types.ts. I'm converting the React logic and components into Blogger's XML structure, including script and style tags. Considering that this is a "Tool Site," I'm thinking of a single-page Blogger template and using Tailwind CDN with custom styles.


**Considering HTML Structure**

I've been mulling over the best approach for the visual aesthetics. I plan to incorporate a background pattern and a glass effect. I have decided to use Vanilla JS over React, due to better Blogger compatibility and faster load times. It's a single-file HTML/JS structure that will be easily pasted into Blogger's HTML section. I also have to remember the custom JSON-LD and OpenGraph tags for a tool site. I am planning a header with home, FAQ, and Blog links.


**Detailing the User Interface**

I'm now deep-diving into the user interface components. I am planning the hero section with a title, URL input, and a green button, followed by the output section with a thumbnail grid and download buttons. I am also planning FAQ content, tool cards, and a footer. I will make sure the RTL display works, based on the `data:blog.languageDirection`.

**Implementing JavaScript Logic**

I've outlined the JavaScript functions I'll need: `extractVideoId`, `fetchVideoMetadata`, `renderThumbnails`, and `handleLanguageChange`. Now I'm working on SEO, and adding JSON-LD for `SoftwareApplication`, and OpenGraph properties to the template. I will ensure AdSense support with placeholders and have a very simple i18n implementation planned.


**Optimizing Download Handling**

I'm now thinking about how to handle the image downloads. It looks like the standard "Open/Save" behavior is the way to go. I'm also preparing the Blogger template, including the XML header, and combining the logic from the React files into Vanilla JS for maximum compatibility. I will be sure that I am optimizing for SEO and the user experience. I am planning the layout of the tool as: Big Hero -> Input -> Results -> Related Tools -> FAQ -> Footer. I also plan to use Tailwind CDN for styles. The interactive elements will be: language switching, fetching, and downloading. Finally, I'll include the JSON-LD to optimize for SEO.


**Building the XML Structure**

I'm now in the process of building the Blogger XML structure. I'm focusing on the `<b:skin>` tag for custom CSS, and the `<body>` for the main content. I'm working to make sure the RTL display works perfectly, using Tailwind's `dir="rtl"` and `data-lang` attributes. I'm using the `Tajawal` font for Arabic. I'll implement `ToolCard` and `FaqItem` with standard JavaScript event listeners. I'm also ensuring the "Download All" button can handle "Open All" functionality.

لتحويل تطبيق "ThumbSnap.io" إلى قالب بلوجر (Blogger Template) كامل وجاهز للعمل، قمت بدمج المنطق البرمجي (React-like logic converted to Vanilla JS for better Blogger performance)، والتصاميم، والترجمات، ومعايير SEO في ملف XML واحد.

إليك الكود الكامل. قم بنسخه بالكامل واستبدال كود القالب الحالي في مدونتك (من خلال المظهر -> تعديل HTML):
There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Transforming ThumbSnap.io**

I'm aiming to convert ThumbSnap.io into a full Blogger template. I've begun to consider the XML structure required, which involves integrating HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Understanding Blogger's server-side rendering and its peculiar tag system are proving challenging, but also fascinating. I'm focusing on how the unique "skin" elements can be best leveraged.


**Defining Template Architecture**

Okay, I've shifted focus to a simpler approach. Building a React-based Blogger template seemed overly complex. Now, I'm leaning toward a clean HTML structure with Vanilla JS or a bundled script. This will involve crafting a single `blogger-template.xml` file, including Blogger boilerplate, Tailwind CSS via CDN, and the React logic, either transpiled or within a script tag, depending on what works best for a self-contained template.


**Revising Approach & Goal**

I'm now prioritizing direct compliance with the user's request. It's about delivering a complete Blogger template, in XML format, without unnecessary additions. I will concentrate on generating the `<b:skin>`, `<b:section>`, and `<CDATA>` components, ensuring the HTML and Javascript are correctly integrated within the XML structure. The goal is to provide a working `.xml` Blogger template based on the source code, as directly and completely as possible.


**Refining XML Integration**

I'm now zeroing in on the file structure; creating `blogger-template.xml` is my priority. My strategy involves embedding CSS and JavaScript logic directly within this XML file. Considering the ThumbSnap.io design and specific requirements, I plan to integrate the React logic, preserving branding elements, HD downloading functionality, and RTL support. I'm focusing on crafting the essential Blogger tags, and also remembering that the user might prefer copy-paste, so I'll create a full-fledged XML template.


**Constructing XML Template**

I'm now focusing on a complete `blogger-template.xml` file. The structure will include the standard XML header and essential Blogger tags. I'll integrate Tailwind and React CDNs directly. The core `App.tsx` logic will be included, and I'll adapt it for browser execution with ESM imports, ensuring I deliver the complete functionality of ThumbSnap.io within the template. This satisfies the user's explicit desire for a *complete* Blogger template.


**Defining Key XML Elements**

I'm now zeroing in on the final deliverable: `blogger-template.xml`. I'll create this single XML file, adapting the provided React code for a self-contained environment. The template will correctly handle `b:skin` and `b:section`, and include logic for Arabic/English toggle and YouTube thumbnail generation. Delivering a valid and functional Blogger template is the focus.

entries.description: A complete, self-contained Blogger XML template that includes all the functionality of ThumbSnap.io, including React-based logic, Tailwind CSS, and multilingual support.

payload.text: عدل اسم الموقع لYTThumbs وايضا ازل الاسم فوق الرابط من الاقواس واجعل اول حرفين باسم الموقع بالاصفر ولا تغير شيء اخر بالموقع نهائيا

**Updating Site Branding** I've decided to rename the site to "YTThumbs." I'm also removing the old name from the subtitle in the hero section, especially in the Arabic translation. My current focus is styling the first two letters of the new name to make them stand out. **Finalizing Logo Implementation** I've successfully updated the logo text in `App.tsx` and the footer, implementing the requested yellow highlighting. Metadata has also been refreshed. Next, I updated the translation dictionaries in `constants.ts`, including the hero title in both English and Arabic. I've removed the bracketed English name from the Arabic hero title, as requested. The next step involves reviewing the changes in the actual site. **Adjusting Title and Metadata** I've updated the title tag in `index.html` to reflect the new site name, "YTThumbs". I also updated the app name in `metadata.json`. Furthermore, I've started the logo styling, ensuring the "YT" in "YTThumbs" is yellow. I'm now updating the translation dictionaries in `constants.ts` with the new site name and removing the bracketed English name in the Arabic hero title, as requested. **Perfecting Brand Styling** I'm now focusing on styling the "YT" in "YTThumbs" to be yellow across the site, especially where the brand is displayed as a logo. I'm manually wrapping "YT" in a span with the `text-yellow-400` class in `App.tsx` for the navigation and hero title. I'm updating the translation strings to just "YTThumbs" and styling the JSX where the hero title is rendered. This aligns with the request to make the first two letters of the site name yellow. I've double checked the instruction to ensure this is what is desired. **Refining SEO and Sitemap** I'm now focused on updating SEO, sitemap, and related files to reflect the new "YTThumbs" branding. This involves adjusting the site name in `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and `constants.ts` (translations). I also need to update the `sitemap.xml` to correctly mention the new site name. I'm verifying the standard Tailwind yellow class, `text-yellow-400`, is used for the logo in the Navbar and Footer, and how to use translations for the hero title. **Optimizing Hero Styling** I'm now revisiting the Hero H1. I'll update the translations to "YTThumbs" and "واي تي ثامبز" without brackets. I'll continue to render the brand logo in the Nav and Footer as `YTThumbs` in `App.tsx`. For the Hero H1, I can split it into "YT" and "Thumbs" if English, but the Arabic title "واي تي" doesn't have "YT" as its first two letters. I will apply the yellow styling logic to the Hero, just like the Nav and Footer. **Clarifying Arabic Logo** I'm now clarifying the Arabic logo display. It seems the goal is to make "YT" yellow everywhere, even if the native letters don't match. I'll proceed accordingly in the XML. This ensures consistent brand visibility across languages, and I'll revisit the Hero H1.

### الموصفات المطلوبة للتعديل:
1.  **تغيير الاسم**: تحويل الاسم من "ThumbSnap.io" إلى "**YTThumbs**".
2.  **التنسيق البصري (Logo)**: جعل أول حرفين (**YT**) باللون الأصفر (`text-yellow-400`) في جميع مواضع الشعار.
3.  **تعديل الترجمة العربية**: إزالة الاسم الأجنبي الموجود بين أقواس في العنوان الرئيسي للغة العربية.
4.  **تحديث البيانات**: تحديث الـ Metadata والـ Sitemap لتعكس الاسم الجديد.

payload.text: اجعل اسم الموقع ثابت YTThumbs حتى اذا تم تغير اللغه

**Maintaining Website Name Identity**

Okay, I've confirmed the crucial need to preserve "YTThumbs" as the website name, even in Arabic. The current Arabic translation, "واي تي ثامبز," will be updated in the `constants.ts` file's `ar` dictionary to ensure consistency. This update is currently in progress. I will make sure the changes reflect across all relevant instances.


**Fixing Arabic Styling Issue**

Alright, I'm now tackling the styling inconsistency in the `App.tsx` file's hero section. My goal is to guarantee that "YTThumbs" maintains its visual style irrespective of language, and I am in the process of ensuring that it stays fixed and doesn't change based on language. I plan to modify the rendering logic to apply the yellow styling universally, and that will be my main focus. I'm making progress, and should have a solution soon!

entries.description: Ensure the site name is YTThumbs in both English and Arabic translations.
Make the stylized YTThumbs logo consistent across all languages in the hero section.

payload.text: عدل التاي الصورة للموقع بالصوره التي رفعتها لك وايضا اجعل المستخدم عند الضغط على اسم الموقع YTThumbs الذي في اعلى الشاشه يعود للشاشه الرئيسيه وايضا اصلح المشكله عند تغير اللغه يتغير حجم الخط بالموقع اجعله ثابت بنفس الخط الذي باللغه الانجليزيه

**Reflecting on Icon Implementation**

I'm now considering the best method to integrate the provided image into the "YTThumbs" application as its logo or icon. My primary focus is on how to represent the image effectively within the application's interface. Specifically, I'm thinking about whether to update a file path or represent the image itself.


**Implementing Directory References**

I've determined I can't directly "upload" a file, and I'll now assume the user will place the icon at `/logo.png`. My code will reference this path for now. I'm focusing on how to represent the path in my code without needing to see the filename the user provides, and I'm using `logo.png` as a placeholder reference.

**Improving Website Navigation**

I've added functionality where clicking the "YTThumbs" name in the navbar will now return the user to the home screen. This resolves the issue with navigation back to the home view. I am happy with the current progress.

**Fixing Arabic Font Consistency**

I'm now prioritizing font consistency across languages. The goal is to keep the font size and style the same between English and Arabic. Instead of different metrics, the user would prefer the same as English. I am checking into using `Inter` for both or ensuring `Tajawal` is sized similarly and matches better.


**Standardizing Font and Logo**

I'm now implementing the logo color and size, and I'll keep "YT" yellow as requested. I've also decided to use `logo.png` within the navbar's `img` tag. The focus is to set the `activeTab` to `'home'` on logo clicks. I'm focusing on consistent font sizes, and, as requested, I'll keep the same font size for both English and Arabic. I'll stick to a unified size class strategy, and the focus is ensuring line heights and base sizes are identical, as this is the best approach to consistent font sizes.


**Optimizing Font Consistency**

I am now evaluating how to apply font consistency across the application. I'm focusing on the request to ensure a constant font size regardless of language. While "Inter" is specified for the body and "Tajawal" for RTL, I'm considering using a single font or a similar size. I've updated the navbar code to include the logo, using the path `logo.png`.


**Prioritizing Font Selection**

I'm now prioritizing the font selection process, as requested, to have the same visual style regardless of language. While initially considering a unified approach using `Inter`, I've observed limitations in its Arabic support. Now, I'll remove the Arabic-specific override, and `Inter` will be the primary font, allowing a fallback that ensures metric consistency. This will align the Arabic font with the English one.


**Revising Brand Implementation**

I'm now making final updates to the brand elements. I've updated `App.tsx` and `index.html` to reflect the logo image, a green square with a landscape icon and page-peel. I'm ensuring the "YT" color in the logo is maintained and that clicking the site name returns the user to the home view, as requested. I've reviewed previous requests, so I'm also addressing the font size consistency issue.

entries.description: Updated font family settings to ensure consistent sizing between English and Arabic by using a unified font stack and removing specific RTL overrides that caused layout shifts.
Added the site logo image, fixed the navbar brand link to return home, and ensured brand consistency in the footer.

payload.text: عند الضغط على اسم الموقع او الصوره للموقع يعمل ريفرش للموقع وايضا اصلح المشكله  عند الضغط على الاسائله الشائعه لا تظهر الاسئله التي بالرئيسيه تظهر كتابات فقط اصلحها ولا تغير شيء اخر

**Improving UX Navigation**

I've been focusing on improving the user experience within `YTThumbs`. My current thoughts revolve around making the navigation more intuitive. Specifically, I'm working to ensure that clicking the site logo or name always returns the user to the home state, essentially refreshing the page. Additionally, I'm considering how best to handle FAQ navigation, trying to move away from a generic page.


**Resolving FAQ Display**

I'm now zeroing in on the FAQ functionality. The current plan involves updating the navbar and footer brand click handlers to refresh the app state. Crucially, I'll revise the FAQ link to display the actual questions and answers directly on the home page, rather than directing to a static text page. My goal is to streamline the UX and meet user expectations by integrating the FAQ content more directly. This involves updating `App.tsx` accordingly.


**Refining FAQ Integration**

I'm now revising how the FAQ is displayed when the "FAQ" tab is selected. The current implementation renders a placeholder. My new approach involves integrating the FAQ section directly into the home page. I'm choosing to either scroll to the existing FAQ section or display it immediately when the 'faq' tab is selected. This involves modifying the logic in `App.tsx` where `activeTab === 'faq'` to ensure the FAQ content, using `FaqItem` components, is rendered instead of placeholder text.


**Fixing Navigation Logic**

I'm now implementing the refresh functionality, so that clicking the site name or image refreshes the page and goes back to `home`. I'm also addressing the font size issue that arises when switching languages. I'll need to check the logic, specifically to account for the image upload request and integrate the previous `index.html` font fix.


**Implementing FAQ Integration**

I'm now focused on directly integrating the FAQ questions into the homepage. My update involves addressing the issue where clicking the FAQ nav link currently shows placeholder text. The new plan modifies `App.tsx` to render the actual FAQ content when the 'faq' tab is active. This ensures the questions and answers display, fixing the previous static text issue. The goal is to provide a seamless user experience, and make the FAQ section immediately available.


**Revising Brand Click**

I've been thinking about the brand click behavior and decided to use state reset instead of `window.location.reload()` for a cleaner refresh within the SPA. My implementation will clear tool results. Also, to improve the FAQ display, I will change the logic to render the actual FAQ content when the 'faq' tab is selected, instead of the placeholder text, fixing the Arabic feedback issue.



**Incorporating Image Logic**

I'm now integrating the image into the site, focusing on the site logo. I'm updating `App.tsx` and `index.html` to display the provided image, or if that is not possible, I'll use a placeholder or high-quality SVG representation for the site logo, ensuring it is clickable. The image includes a green square with a white frame and a yellow sun element.

entries.description: Ensure consistent font family and sizing across languages to prevent layout shifts when switching to Arabic.
Modified brand click to trigger a full home reset (refresh), and updated FAQ tab to show the actual interactive FAQ components instead of placeholder text.

payload.text:
احذف هذه الادوات التي بالرئيسيه 📝
مولد عناوين يوتيوب
AI-powered high CTR titles.

افتح الأداة
🏷️
مستخرج الكلمات المفتاحية
Extract viral keywords instantly.

افتح الأداة
📐
دليل أحجام الغلاف
Perfect dimensions for all devices.

افتح الأداة
📱
نصائح الفيديوهات القصيرة
Master the vertical video game.

افتح الأداة وايضا احذف اي شيء متعلق بها وايضا اجعل المدونة تظهر مدونات عن الموقع ومواضيع مشابهه حصريه

**Updating the Home Page**

I'm currently focused on removing the "Related Tools" section from the Home page. It feels extraneous, and I'm ready to take it out. Furthermore, I'm thinking I'll flesh out the "Blog" tab with actual content. I'm imagining it will feature topics like YouTube growth strategies, design tips, and thumbnail optimization, which will be much more helpful to visitors.


**Structuring Blog Articles**

I've made progress on the blog section! I'm removing the "Related Tools" from the Home page. I'm now replacing the string with some actual content. I've structured three starter articles about the site's niche, like video thumbnails and image formats. I'm imagining a structured format with titles and content. The visual design is going to be clean.


**Refining Blog Display**

I'm now streamlining the blog display logic. My goal is to use the existing `activeTab === 'blog'` section within `App.tsx` instead of creating a new `renderBlog` function. I've updated the logic to display actual blog content. I've also removed the `ToolCard` component and its associated rendering from the codebase.

entries.description: Remove the Tools section from the Home page and enhance the Blog section with exclusive articles related to YouTube thumbnails and channel growth.

payload.text: عدل سياسه الخصوصيه لجعلها مناسبه للموقع وقويه وتنقبل في ادسنس

**Revising Policy Content**

I'm currently focused on the Privacy Policy, aiming for robustness, YTThumbs suitability, and Google AdSense compliance. My priority is getting it right in both Arabic and English. I’m making good progress, and I’m ready to move onto the next step.


**Drafting Privacy Policy Details**

I've outlined the core sections for the privacy policy, focusing on compliance with AdSense and relevant data privacy laws like CCPA, GDPR, and COPPA. I’ve drafted an introduction, data collection and cookies, third-party links, AdSense/Advertising and children's privacy. I also included updates and contact sections. The plan is to translate the policy to Arabic professionally. I'll modify `App.tsx` and the `renderOther` call.


**Improving Policy Formatting**

I'm thinking now about how to improve the formatting of the policy details to ensure it's structured well. The simple line split may not meet the need for a strong policy. I've also added some important notes: a disclaimer about non-affiliation with YouTube, a mention of Google as a third-party vendor, and detail about opt-out options for DART cookies.

entries.description: Update Privacy Policy to be professional, robust, and AdSense-compliant, including details about cookies, third-party advertising, and data handling. Also updated Terms of Service for better clarity.

payload.text:
عدّل وأضف على موقعي جميع عناصر تحسين الظهور في محركات البحث (SEO) وفق أفضل ممارسات Google، مع الالتزام بالنقاط التالية بدقة:

1) إنشاء ملف sitemap.xml ديناميكي يحتوي جميع الصفحات القابلة للفهرسة، ويتم تحديثه تلقائيًا عند إضافة أي صفحة جديدة، ووضعه في المسار الجذري للموقع، مع ربطه داخل robots.txt.

2) إنشاء ملف robots.txt صحيح يسمح بأرشفة الموقع بالكامل، ويتضمن رابط sitemap.xml بشكل صريح.

3) تحسين <title> لكل صفحة بحيث يكون فريدًا، غنيًا بالكلمات المفتاحية، غير مكرر، وطوله مناسب للظهور في نتائج البحث.

4) إضافة meta description احترافي لكل صفحة، جذاب لزيادة CTR، بطول مناسب، ويشرح وظيفة الصفحة بوضوح.

5) تنظيم العناوين داخل الصفحات باستخدام:
   - H1 واحد فقط لكل صفحة
   - استخدام H2 و H3 بشكل منطقي لدعم المحتوى
   - عدم تكرار H1

6) إضافة محتوى نصي حقيقي أسفل الأداة أو في الصفحة الرئيسية يشرح:
   - ما الذي يقدمه الموقع
   - طريقة الاستخدام
   - لماذا الموقع مفيد
   - أسئلة شائعة (FAQ) لتحسين الفهرسة

7) تحسين بنية الروابط لتكون SEO Friendly بدون باراميترات غير ضرورية، وباستخدام كلمات مفتاحية واضحة.

8) تحسين جميع الصور عبر:
   - إضافة alt نصي وصفي
   - استخدام أسماء ملفات واضحة
   - ضغط الصور دون فقدان الجودة
   - منع استخدام الصور بدون وصف

9) التأكد من أن الموقع سريع ومتوافق مع Core Web Vitals:
   - تقليل حجم JavaScript و CSS
   - تحميل كسول للصور (Lazy Load)
   - عدم وجود أخطاء تعيق التحميل

10) التأكد من أن التصميم متجاوب 100% مع الهواتف (Mobile First) وجميع العناصر تعمل بشكل مثالي على الشاشات الصغيرة.

11) إضافة دعم HTTPS بشكل كامل وعدم وجود أي محتوى مختلط (Mixed Content).

12) إضافة صفحات إلزامية لزيادة الثقة:
   - Privacy Policy
   - Terms of Service
   - About Us
   - Contact Us

13) إضافة Structured Data (Schema):
   - نوع WebApplication أو SoftwareApplication
   - FAQ Schema للأسئلة الشائعة
   - تحسين ظهور الموقع في النتائج الغنية (Rich Results)

14) تحسين تجربة المستخدم:
   - واجهة بسيطة وواضحة
   - أزرار واضحة
   - مسار استخدام سهل
   - تقليل الإعلانات المزعجة

15) التأكد من أن الموقع جاهز للإضافة في Google Search Console بدون أخطاء فهرسة.

نفّذ جميع التعديلات باحتراف، دون كسر وظائف الموقع الحالية، وراعِ أن يكون الموقع مناسبًا للأدوات والخدمات ذات الترافيك العالي.

**Optimizing Site Structure**

I'm currently focused on the `index.html` file, specifically the implementation of SEO best practices. I'm prioritizing the refinement of meta tags, social media tags, and structured data, aiming for comprehensive coverage. I intend to enhance its current status by improving key performance factors.


**Refining On-Page SEO**

I've made progress on page structure in `App.tsx`, focusing on unique H1s and adding "About Us" and "Contact Us" pages. I'm dynamically implementing FAQ Schema and ensuring lazy loading. I'm updating translations in `constants.ts` for the new sections and SEO-rich text. The `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt` are checked, and unique `<title>` tags are dynamically handled. I'm now focused on dynamic `<meta description>` updates and improving content within the tabs.


**Prioritizing Homepage Content**

I'm now shifting focus to making the homepage content extremely rich to target primary keywords, using "Best YouTube Thumbnail Downloader HD" as my H1. I'm prioritizing adding image `alt` tags and lazy loading, expanding Structured Data with FAQ and Review Schema, and integrating essential pages like Privacy and Terms. To optimize, I'm aiming for minimal code for performance. I'll also modify `App.tsx` for dynamic meta descriptions and update translations for new content.

entries.description: Comprehensive SEO overhaul: Updated meta tags for better search visibility, added dynamic Open Graph tags, and refined the structured data for SoftwareApplication and FAQ patterns.
Expanded TranslationDict to include About Us and Contact Us sections for improved SEO and trust.
Updated translations with SEO-rich content for all sections including About and Contact.

Refactored App structure for SEO: Added About Us and Contact Us sections, ensured proper H1/H2 usage per view, implemented dynamic metadata updates, and added structured FAQ schema. Improved image loading with lazy loading.
Updated dynamic sitemap with all core pages.

payload.text:
عدل التالي في الموقع بالكامل هذه دومين موقعي 
ytthumbs.fun

**Updating Domain Name**

I'm currently focused on the domain name update. I've identified that `ytthumbs.io` appears in the `index.html` file, specifically in meta tags. I need to make the change to `ytthumbs.fun` in the `index.html` file.


**Reviewing Domain Updates**

I'm now diving deeper into the required domain updates. My focus has shifted to systematically reviewing the files to ensure the changes are correct. I'm scrutinizing `index.html` particularly, and am updating the canonical link, og:url, og:image, twitter:url, and twitter:image to `ytthumbs.fun` from `ytthumbs.io`.

Updated sitemap link to use ytthumbs.fun.

payload.text: هناك كلمات عند تحويل الموقع عربي تفضل انجليزي عدلها وايضا الموقع عند ترجمته يتغير معاني الكلام اجعلها ثابته

**Addressing Language Issues**

I'm currently focused on resolving the remaining English words on the Arabic version of `ytthumbs.fun`, specifically addressing the translation inconsistencies I've identified. It appears some words are stubbornly resisting the language switch, and I'm also grappling with "translation shifts" that alter the intended meaning in different languages.


**Refining Terminology Accuracy**

I'm now diving into `constants.ts` to improve Arabic phrasing, aiming for a more professional and stable tone across the board. I'm focusing on the `THUMBNAIL_QUALITIES` labels as well, and ensuring consistent usage and accurate translation. Also, I'm checking for translation consistency, focusing on "Advertisement," "Format," "JPG," "WEBP," "Ultra HD," and other hardcoded labels, ensuring they are correctly translated. I am also double checking default title "YouTube Video".


**Integrating Translation Dictionaries**

I'm now integrating thumbnail quality labels and the "YouTube Video" default title into the translation dictionary. I've realized `fetchVideoMetadata` pulls quality labels from `THUMBNAIL_QUALITIES`, requiring this crucial update. This involves updating `App.tsx` and refactoring `fetchVideoMetadata`. Specifically, I am updating the Nav/Footer text. I plan to handle the default title in UI or from the translation dict. I'm focusing on "Advertisement", "Reserved Space", "Home", "Resources", "Legal", "Company", etc.


**Updating Thumbnail Labels**

I've just added the new thumbnail labels and related keys to `TranslationDict` in `types.ts`, and incorporated updates in `constants.ts` and `App.tsx` to align with the translation dictionary. I've also added several new labels in the dictionary for "Download All", "Download", "Open HD", "Dimensions", and "Format". Finally, I'm working on a critical fix: instead of using `thumb.label` directly, I need to look up these values from the translation dictionary to solve the static array issue.

تحياتي، بصفتي مهندس واجهات أمامية خبير، سأقوم بتعديل الموقع بالكامل لضمان تعريب احترافي وشامل (Localization) مع تثبيت المعاني لمنع أي لبس ناتج عن الترجمة التلقائية.

### خطة العمل:
1.  **تحديث `types.ts`**: إضافة مفاتيح جديدة للكلمات التي كانت تظهر بالإنجليزية (مثل تسميات جودة الصور، نصوص الإعلانات، وأقسام التذييل).
2.  **تحديث `constants.ts`**: تعريب تسميات الجودة (Ultra HD، Standard، إلخ) داخل قاموس الترجمة لضمان ثباتها.
3.  **تعديل `App.tsx`**: 
    *   استبدال كافة النصوص الصلبة (Hardcoded strings) بمفاتيح من قاموس الترجمة.
    *   تعريب قسم "كيفية الاستخدام" بالكامل.
    *   تعريب نصوص الإعلانات "Advertisement".
    *   تحسين صياغة سياسة الخصوصية ومن نحن لتكون لغة عربية سليمة وقوية.
4.  **تحديث `AdPlaceholder.tsx`**: ليدعم الترجمة بناءً على اللغة المختارة.

إليك الكود المحدث:

entries.description: Added missing translation keys for quality labels, ads, and home instructions to ensure full localization.
Updated TRANSLATIONS with fixed quality labels and footer section names to prevent English words from appearing in Arabic mode.
Simplified fetchVideoMetadata to use generic quality IDs that map to localized translation keys.
Localized AdPlaceholder component to support Arabic labels.
Refactored App.tsx to remove all hardcoded English strings and use the new translation keys for full Arabic compatibility.
